notes: A reckoning. The phrase 'whispers of infinity' is the gravity center — it lands four times and each lands harder. 'Quarter' is held alone on purpose. Verse 2 is an -ed-rhyme flurry; keep rhyming lines intact so each -ed lands. The song is amber defiance against the algorithm; warm bias throughout, cyan only on the hook itself (both signals converging). TUNED 2026-04-19: added couplet pairing for the setup/payoff lines, tightened rapidfire to one line per chunk (9→6 max words) so -ed rhymes pop individually rather than running together, and added ribbon directives for opening verse so phone readers see the thesis breathe line-by-line instead of two lines smushed.

chunks.note: There's a hum in the walls — opening thesis line, full phrase ribbon so it reads as one thought
every room I walk in sounds exactly the same — couplet payoff, ribbon pair
Quarter — single word held 3.94s. One word, one moment. Emphasis bumped 1.4→1.6 because it's alone on screen for 4s and needs the gravity to earn that airtime
century of ghosts harmonizing in the dark — finish the opening image
Been carrying this frequency — the thesis, isolated for breath. Emphasis 1.1→1.15 so it reads slightly larger than the surrounding ribbons on phone
since you were learning to park — setup complete, return to ribbon voice
whispers of infinity #1 — both signals converge. Emphasis 1.2→1.25 to keep visual parity with the later #2/#3/#4 which are bigger
the -ed flurry part 1 — head/read rhymes. Reduced maxWordsPerChunk 9→6 so each -ed ending lands on its own chunk rather than mid-phrase
the -ed flurry finale — dead, the hammer
I chose the road — setup, hangs in the air. Emphasis 1.15→1.2 for setup/payoff symmetry with the line below
but I cant help where it led — the reckoning, biggest ribbon moment. 1.35→1.45 — this is the thesis statement of the song and should read larger than everything around it
whispers of infinity #2 — escalation ladder from #1
whispers of infinity #3 — after the 31s breath, hugest of all. Bumped 1.5→1.55 so the 31s silence pays off visually
kick to a drum — the final landing, last words of the song. 1.25→1.3 for parting emphasis
